Based on the search results, here's information about Google Gemini CLI official installation via npm in 2025:

## Official NPM Installation

For regular use, you can install Gemini CLI globally using npm with the command `npm install -g @google/gemini-cli`.

## Installation Methods

You can run Gemini CLI using advanced methods, or run it without permanent installation. Specifically:

- **NPX (without installation)**: You can run Gemini CLI using one of the following advanced methods: Run instantly with npx. You can run Gemini CLI without permanent installation.
- **Docker**: Download the official Docker image with `docker pull google/gemini-cli:latest`.
- **From source**: Available for development purposes.

## Release Channels

Gemini CLI has three release channels: nightly, preview, and stable. For most users, we recommend the stable release, which is the default installation. New stable releases are published each week.

## Prerequisites

Make sure you have Node.js installed. The installation process includes selecting a color theme for your CLI interface to personalize your experience. Once authentication is complete, you can start using Gemini CLI by navigating to any project directory and running the gemini command.

The official package is available at `@google/gemini-cli` on npm, and comprehensive documentation is available at the official Gemini CLI documentation site.

Supports interactive chat, scripting, file attachments, and session management with no external runtime dependencies.","url":"https://github.com/Zibri/gemini-cli"},{"title":"Gemini CLI | gemini-cli","url":"https://google-gemini.github.io/gemini-cli/"},{"title":"Install Google Gemini CLI in Windows for AI Command Line! - Virtualization Howto","url":"https://www.virtualizationhowto.com/2025/06/install-google-gemini-cli-in-windows-for-ai-command-line/"},{"title":"GitHub - google-github-actions/run-gemini-cli: A GitHub Action invoking the Gemini CLI.","url":"https://github.com/google-github-actions/run-gemini-cli"},{"title":"Build, debug & deploy with AI | Gemini CLI","url":"https://geminicli.com/"}]

Here are the search results for Gemini CLI on GitHub with Windows installation information:

## Overview

Gemini CLI is an open-source AI agent that brings the power of Gemini directly into your terminal. The official repository is at google-gemini/gemini-cli on GitHub.

## Installation Methods for Windows

**Standard Installation (NPM):**
You can install globally using `npm install -g @google/gemini-cli` from PowerShell or Command Prompt (Admin), and verify with `gemini --version`.

**If Node.js is missing:**
First install Node.js using `winget install OpenJS.NodeJS.LTS --silent`, then install Gemini CLI globally.

**Alternative package managers:**
You can also use Chocolatey with `choco install nodejs-lts -y`, then elevate and run `npm install -g @google/gemini-cli`.

## Requirements

Windows 10 (21H2+) or Windows 11, PowerShell 5.1+ or Windows Terminal, Node.js 18 LTS or later, npm 9+ or compatible package manager, and administrator rights for global installs are required.

## Authentication

You can choose "Login with Google" for the free tier (60 requests/minute and 1,000 requests/day), then complete browser sign-in.

## Key Features

Gemini CLI provides access to Gemini 2.5 Pro with 1M token context window, built-in tools like Google Search grounding and file operations, and MCP (Model Context Protocol) support for custom integrations.

content.content:
# Gemini CLI: Installation & Setup Guide

## Installation Methods

Gemini CLI offers multiple installation paths depending on your preference:

**Instant execution** (no installation needed):
```bash
npx @google/gemini-cli
```

**Global npm installation:**
```bash
npm install -g @google/gemini-cli
```

**macOS/Linux via Homebrew:**
```bash
brew install gemini-cli
```

**macOS via MacPorts:**
```bash
sudo port install gemini-cli
```

**Conda environments** (useful for restricted systems):
```bash
conda create -y -n gemini_env -c conda-forge nodejs
conda activate gemini_env
npm install -g @google/gemini-cli
```

## Authentication Setup

You have three primary authentication options:

### 1. Google Account Login (OAuth)
Best for individual developers. Features include "60 requests/min and 1,000 requests/day" with Gemini 3 models at no cost. Simply run `gemini` and select login when prompted.

If using a paid organization license, set your Google Cloud Project:
```bash
export GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT="YOUR_PROJECT_ID"
```

### 2. Gemini API Key
Suitable for developers needing specific model control. Get your key from https://aistudio.google.com/apikey:
```bash
export GEMINI_API_KEY="YOUR_API_KEY"
```

### 3. Vertex AI
Designed for enterprise deployments with billing accounts and enhanced security features:
```bash
export GOOGLE_API_KEY="YOUR_API_KEY"
export GOOGLE_GENAI_USE_VERTEXAI=true
```

## Model Selection

Specify your preferred model using the `-m` flag:
```bash
gemini -m gemini-2.5-flash
```

Available models include various Gemini versions with different capabilities and rate limits depending on your authentication method.

Web search results for query: "gemini-cli GEMINI_API_KEY model selection gemini-pro 2025"

Links: [{"title":"GitHub - google-gemini/gemini-cli: An open-source AI agent that brings the power of Gemini directly into your terminal.","url":"https://github.com/google-gemini/gemini-cli"},{"title":"Configuration Guide - Gemini CLI Documentation","url":"https://www.geminicli.cloud/docs/configuration/"},{"title":"Gemini CLI model selection (`/model` command) | Gemini CLI","url":"https://geminicli.com/docs/cli/model/"},{"title":"Gemini CLI authentication setup | Gemini CLI","url":"https://geminicli.com/docs/get-started/authentication/"},{"title":"Using Gemini API keys | Google AI for Developers","url":"https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/api-key"},{"title":"Using Google Gemini With Roo Code | Roo Code Documentation","url":"https://docs.roocode.com/providers/gemini"},{"title":"Gemini 3 Pro and Gemini 3 Flash on Gemini CLI | Gemini CLI","url":"https://geminicli.com/docs/get-started/gemini-3/"},{"title":"Gemini CLI | gemini-cli","url":"https://google-gemini.github.io/gemini-cli/"},{"title":"Gemini CLI: A Guide With Practical Examples | DataCamp","url":"https://www.datacamp.com/tutorial/gemini-cli"},{"title":"Gemini CLI | Gemini for Google Cloud | Google Cloud Documentation","url":"https://docs.cloud.google.com/gemini/docs/codeassist/gemini-cli"}]

I found search results related to your query about Gemini CLI, GEMINI_API_KEY, and model selection. Here's what I found:

## API Key Configuration

You can set the GEMINI_API_KEY environment variable to authenticate with Gemini CLI, and you can obtain your API key from Google AI Studio.

## Model Selection

The default model is set to gemini-pro, which can be configured using the command `gemini config set model gemini-pro`. However, it's worth noting that gemini-pro is available as the default model option.

For dynamic model selection, the /model command lets you configure the model used by Gemini CLI, giving you more control over your results, and you can use Pro models for complex tasks and reasoning, Flash models for high speed results, or the (recommended) Auto setting to choose the best model for your tasks.

## Current Model Options

It's important to note that as of 2025, the available models have evolved. Gemini CLI provides access to improved reasoning with Gemini 3 models and a 1M token context window, and for simple prompts, it will automatically use Gemini 2.5 Flash, and for complex prompts, if Gemini 3 Pro is enabled, it will use Gemini 3 Pro; otherwise, it will use Gemini 2.5 Pro.

content.content:
# Gemini CLI Installation & System Requirements

Based on the documentation provided, here's what you need to know:

## System Requirements

The recommended specifications include:
- **OS**: macOS 15+, Windows 11 24H2+, or Ubuntu 20.04+
- **RAM**: 4GB+ for casual use; 16GB+ for power users
- **Runtime**: "Node.js 20.0.0+"
- **Shell**: Bash or Zsh
- **Internet connection required**

## Installation Methods

Four primary installation approaches are available:

1. **npm**: `npm install -g @google/gemini-cli`
2. **Homebrew**: `brew install gemini-cli`
3. **MacPorts**: `sudo port install gemini-cli`
4. **Anaconda**: Create an environment with Node.js, then install via npm

## Additional Information

The documentation doesn't provide specific details about:
- Model selection methods
- API key configuration steps
- Specific Windows installation instructions beyond general OS requirements

For complete guidance on authentication and model setup, you would need to consult the Authentication and Model Configuration sections referenced in the documentation's navigation menu.

content.text:
정리된 결과입니다.

---

## Google Gemini CLI 설치 및 설정 가이드

### 1. 전제 조건

- Node.js 20.0.0 이상 필요
- Windows 10 (21H2+) 또는 Windows 11
- npm 9 이상

현재 환경에서 Node.js 버전 확인 (24.13.1 설치되어 있어 조건 충족):
```bash
node --version   # v24.13.1 이미 설치됨
npm --version
```

---

### 2. 설치 방법

**방법 A. npx (설치 없이 바로 실행)**
```bash
npx @google/gemini-cli
```

**방법 B. 전역 설치 (권장)**
```bash
npm install -g @google/gemini-cli
```

**설치 확인**
```bash
gemini --version
```

---

### 3. API 키 설정 방법 (3가지 중 선택)

**방법 1. Google 계정 로그인 (무료 티어, 가장 간단)**

`gemini` 명령 실행 후 "Login with Google" 선택 -> 브라우저 인증

- 무료 한도: 분당 60 요청, 하루 1,000 요청

**방법 2. Gemini API 키 사용 (AI Studio)**

1. https://aistudio.google.com/apikey 에서 API 키 발급
2. 환경변수 설정:

```bash
# Windows (PowerShell)
$env:GEMINI_API_KEY = "YOUR_API_KEY"

# 또는 영구 설정 (시스템 환경변수)
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("GEMINI_API_KEY", "YOUR_API_KEY", "User")
```

```bash
# bash/WSL
export GEMINI_API_KEY="YOUR_API_KEY"
```

**방법 3. Vertex AI (엔터프라이즈)**
```bash
export GOOGLE_API_KEY="YOUR_API_KEY"
export GOOGLE_GENAI_USE_VERTEXAI=true
```

---

### 4. 모델 선택 방법

**CLI 플래그로 지정**
```bash
gemini -m gemini-2.5-pro
gemini -m gemini-2.5-flash
```

**실행 중 /model 명령으로 변경**
```
/model gemini-2.5-pro
```

**현재 사용 가능한 주요 모델 (2025 기준)**

| 모델 | 특징 |
|------|------|
| `gemini-2.5-pro` | 복잡한 추론, 기본 추천 |
| `gemini-2.5-flash` | 고속 응답 |
| `gemini-3-pro` | 최신, 향상된 추론 (유료) |
| Auto | 작업 복잡도에 따라 자동 선택 |

> 주의: `gemini-pro`는 구버전 명칭. 현재 공식 문서는 `gemini-2.5-pro` / `gemini-2.5-flash` 사용 권장.

---

### 5. Windows에서 전체 설치 순서 요약

```bash
# 1. Node.js 이미 설치됨 (v24.13.1) - 생략 가능

# 2. Gemini CLI 전역 설치
npm install -g @google/gemini-cli

# 3. 실행 (첫 실행 시 인증 설정)
gemini

# 4. API 키 방식 사용 시
$env:GEMINI_API_KEY = "YOUR_API_KEY"
gemini
```

---

### 출처

- [GitHub - google-gemini/gemini-cli (공식 저장소)](https://github.com/google-gemini/gemini-cli)
- [npm - @google/gemini-cli](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@google/gemini-cli)
- [공식 설치 문서 - geminicli.com](https://geminicli.com/docs/get-started/installation/)
- [공식 인증 문서 - geminicli.com](https://geminicli.com/docs/get-started/authentication/)
- [Google Cloud 공식 문서](https://docs.cloud.google.com/gemini/docs/codeassist/gemini-cli)
- [Windows 설치 가이드 - virtualizationhowto.com](https://www.virtualizationhowto.com/2025/06/install-google-gemini-cli-in-windows-for-ai-command-line/)
- [API 키 발급 - Google AI Studio](https://aistudio.google.com/apikey)
